data.title: BABA Earnings: Alibaba Stock Falls 5% on Earnings Miss, Even as AI Cloud Jumps 36%
data.description:
Alibaba (BABA) reported weaker-than-expected results for its Q3 FY26, sending the stock down about 5% in premarket trading. The company missed analyst estimates on both earnings and revenue, as rising costs offset strong growth in its AI and cloud business.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Alibaba reported earnings of RMB 7.09 per ADS, missing the analyst estimate of RMB 10.94. Revenue came in at RMB 284.84 billion (about $40.7 billion), up 2% year over year but below expectations of RMB 289.3 billion.

AI and Cloud Growth Remain Strong

Despite the weak headline numbers, Alibaba's Cloud Intelligence unit remained a bright spot. Revenue in the segment grew 36%, with AI-related products posting triple-digit growth for the tenth straight quarter.

The company also said its Qwen AI app has crossed 300 million monthly active users, showing strong traction in its AI push.

Margin and Spending Concerns Remain

Higher costs remain the key issue. Alibaba is spending heavily to compete with PDD Holdings (PDD) and JD.com (JD) in China's e-commerce market. Investments in fast delivery, discounts, and user incentives reduced adjusted EBITA by about 45%. Adjusted EBITA also fell 57% during the quarter.

At the same time, the company is continuing its large AI investment plan, with management sticking to a RMB 380 billion spending target over three years.

Guidance Signals Continued Heavy Investment

CEO Eddie Wu said Alibaba will stay in a "high-intensity" investment phase over the next three years. The company is maintaining its RMB 380 billion ($53 billion) AI spending plan through 2027.

Management expects Fiscal 2026 revenue to grow about 6% to roughly $148 billion. It also aims to improve cloud margins over time as AI products scale.

Is Alibaba Stock a Good Buy Right Now?  

Analysts remain bullish about Alibaba's stock trajectory. With eight Buy ratings and one Hold rating, BABA stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating on TipRanks. Also, the average Alibaba price target of $197.86 implies about 47.18% upside potential from current levels. However, it's worth noting that estimates will likely change following today's earnings report.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Did Broadcom (AVGO) Insiders Sell $88M in Company Shares?
data.description:
Multiple insiders at chipmaker Broadcom (AVGO) sold shares totaling $88.10 million from March 16-18, sparking investor concerns despite the company's strong Q1 FY26 results. These sales occurred as AVGO shares declined 1.7% yesterday and 5.4% over the past month, amid a broader market sell-off.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A corporate insider's share sale usually signals caution about the company's future potential, though they often stem from other reasons like financial or personal obligations. Hence, it is important to watch these trades for clues on the company's growth expectations.

A Closer Look at the Insider Transactions

Mark David Brazeal, Broadcom's Chief Legal & Corp. Affairs Officer sold 83,682 AVGO shares for roughly $27.06 million at weighted average prices ranging between $318.764 and $328.841 apiece.

ISG President S. Ram Velaga sold 64,019 Broadcom shares for $20.70 million in multiple transactions at weighted average prices ranging between $317.946 and $328.766 apiece.

Additionally, SSG President Charlie Kawwas and CFO & CAO Kirsten M. Spears sold $20.93 million and $19.41 million worth of Broadcom shares, respectively.

The sales were disclosed through Form 4 filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Following the latest share sales, the individual insiders still held notable shares in the company. As noted in the filings, these shares were sold via automatic transactions to cover withholding taxes due upon the vesting of restricted stock units (RSUs), as required under the relevant RSU awards.

AVGO stock currently has a Very Negative Insider Confidence Signal on TipRanks, based on Informative Sell transactions worth $231.6 million, undertaken in the last three months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Cohen & Steers Sees Opportunities Among Natural Resources
data.description:
Tyler Rosenlicht, a portfolio manager and the head of Natural Resource Equities at Cohen & Steers took the stage at VettaFi-sponsored Exchange event in Las Vegas this week in an info session titled "Beyond Market Concentration: Active ETFs for Today's Market" to discuss some of the opportunities while also laying out the case for active management in the current market environment.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Investors are "Way Under-Allocated to Things that do Well in Inflationary Environments" 

Among the ETFs offered by Cohen & Steers, Rosenlicht recommends that investors consider Natural Resources Active ETF (CSNR) as he sees the current positioning within natural resources to be "way under-allocated."  A decade-long period from 2009 to 2020 when inflation consistently came in below expectations has led to widespread complacency around inflation risk and limited portfolio protection against it, but the recent market behavior – 2022 asset class performance, for instance, when equities fell roughly 18% and fixed income dropped 16% – highlights the importance of diversification, Rosenlicht noted.  This is especially true now that the investment landscape is shifting from an "era of abundance" into an "era of scarcity," he stated.

The portfolio manager added that years of underinvestment across the resource economy – spanning energy, metals, rare earths, uranium, and agriculture – have created significant supply constraints. At the same time, global growth is becoming increasingly resource- and energy-intensive, especially with the rise of technologies like AI, and this is driving strong demand for electricity and key inputs such as copper, lithium, and uranium, while supply struggles to keep pace, tightening overall market dynamics, Rosenlicht noted.

Holdings, Performance, Relative Ranking

According TipRanks' data, Exxon Mobil (XOM), Shell (SHEL), TotalEnergies (TTE), Bunge Global (BG) and Suncor (SU) are the fund's top-5 holdings. Two of the names, Bunge Global and Suncor, have earned TipRanks' highest Smart Score.
data.author: Gary Deduke

data.title: Samsung Plans $74B AI War Chest, Eyes Reorganization — What Does This Mean for SSNLF Stock?
data.description:
South Korean chipmaker and foundry Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) on Thursday pulled back the curtain on its Corporate Value Enhancement plan, aiming to pump over KRW 110 trillion ($73.5 billion) in investment into its facilities as well as research and development in this year alone.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Samsung to Prioritize Reorganization, M&As

With the company seeking to "secure [its] leadership in the AI semiconductor era," SSNLF stock could be headed for even more significant upsides, especially as key partner, the chipmaking giant Nvidia (NVDA), has forecast an impressive $1 trillion in order bookings through 2027.

Samsung, which dominates the global memory chip market, in a corporate filing also stated plans to reorganize its business structure. The Suwon-based company plans to use this to target "future-oriented businesses" in areas such as AI and advanced robotics to keep its growth sustainable in the mid-to-long-term.

In addition, Samsung said it will chase "meaningful" opportunities for mergers and acquisitions in these future growth sectors and others such as medical technology, automotive electronics, and heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: SanDisk Stock (SNDK) Is Down 5% In Pre-Market Today. Is the Dip a "Gift" for Investors?
data.description:
SanDisk (SNDK) stock trended 5% lower in pre-market trading on Thursday after closing at a record high of $753.69 on Wednesday. The drop was not driven by any company-specific news. Instead, it followed Micron's (MU) earnings, which pulled down sentiment across the chip sector.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It is common for investors to react to one major company and adjust positions across the entire space.

Have high conviction in SNDK? Leverage your view

Why This Dip Looks Temporary

  • Micron's Spending vs. SanDisk's Focus: A primary reason for the sector-wide jitters was Micron's announcement of massive new capital expenditure (CapEx) to build out new factories. While Micron is currently in a heavy building phase that eats into immediate cash flow, SanDisk has already moved past its major infrastructure and structural shifts following its spinoff from Western Digital (WDC) in February 2026. This allows SanDisk to stay leaner and focus its capital on maximizing profit margins right now, rather than massive construction costs.

  • The "Stargate" Advantage: SanDisk's 128TB SSDs, built on its "Stargate" platform, are currently being qualified by major cloud providers. These drives use newer QLC technology to improve storage density and efficiency, which is important for AI data centers. While still ramping, this gives SanDisk a strong position as demand for high-capacity storage continues to grow.

  • Locked-In Revenue: SanDisk is moving toward multi-year deals with large cloud customers, with some agreements already in place and more under discussion. This gives better visibility into future demand and helps reduce the usual swings seen in memory stocks.

The Bottom Line for Investors

SanDisk reached an all-time high, which shows that investors are recognizing its role in the AI market. The recent dip appears to be driven by broader sector movement rather than any weakness in the company.

For those who missed the 217% YTD gain and the massive 1,237% rally in 2025, this modest pullback offers a rare chance to consider a market leader at a slight discount.

What Is the Price Target for SanDisk?

Wall Street analysts currently rate SNDK stock a Strong Buy based on 12 Buys, three Holds, and zero Sells. The average SNDK price target of $688.33 indicates an 8.67% downside potential over the next 12 months.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: BLackRock's iShares Global Energy ETF (IXC):  This Energy ETF Could Be a Smart 2026 Hedge amid Spiking Oil Prices
data.description:
As Brent crude prices surge toward $116/bbl following the infrastructure attacks in Qatar, investors are increasingly seeking ways to mitigate risk. While high-growth tech stocks remain volatile, BlackRock's (BLK) iShares Global Energy ETF (IXC) is gaining attention as a defensive option.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

IXC gives exposure to global oil and gas leaders, making it a useful tool during uncertain times. With energy prices staying firm and geopolitical risks still in play, the fund is increasingly seen as a way to balance portfolios.

A Natural Hedge Against Global Uncertainty

IXC tends to benefit when energy prices rise, especially during supply disruptions or geopolitical tensions. The fund holds global majors like Exxon Mobil (XOM), Chevron (CVX), and Shell (SHEL), offering broader protection than U.S.-only energy ETFs.

It also provides a dividend yield of about 2.8%, adding steady income alongside price support. This can help offset weakness in other sectors during volatile periods.

Currently, IXC is trading at $55.14, which is essentially right at its 52-week high of $55.49, which is often viewed as a sign of strength. While other sectors have seen outflows, energy has attracted steady inflows, suggesting investors expect demand to remain strong into 2026.

What Does Wall Street Expect?

According to TipRanks data, IXC has a Moderate Buy consensus based on 50 analyst ratings. The IXC average price target of $53.45 suggests a slight downside of about 3% from current levels, indicating limited near-term upside but stable expectations overall.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: FedEx (FDX) Q3 Earnings Today – Estimates and Key KPIs to Watch
data.description:
FedEx (FDX) will release its third-quarter fiscal 2026 results after market close today, March 19. Investors should monitor consensus expectations and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) ahead of results, as they serve as important benchmarks for gauging both market sentiment and company performance, with beats or misses often driving sharp stock price reactions.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

FedEx is a global leader in transportation, e-commerce, and logistics services. It handles package delivery, freight shipping, and related business solutions worldwide. Fuel remains its most unpredictable cost; recent oil surges past $100 from Iran tensions threaten margins, while U.S.-Israeli strikes and Iran's cargo ship attacks risk pandemic-like shipping disruptions for retailers and supply chains.

Expectations from FedEx

The Street expects FedEx to post earnings per share (EPS) of $4.15, down from $4.51 reported last year. Sales are forecast to rise 6% year-over-year to $23.49 billion.

For FedEx, Average Daily Package Volume and Average Daily Freight Pounds stand out as two vital KPIs. Package volume tracks shipment counts to boost efficiency in sorting and delivery networks, while freight pounds measure weight for air/ground capacity planning.

Why Average Daily Package Volume Is Important

Average Daily Package Volume tracks the average number of individual parcels FedEx processes each day, primarily via its Express and Ground networks for standard small-shipment deliveries. This metric indicates the demand trends and operational scale.

After a Q2 2025 dip, it shows slight recovery from strong U.S. domestic performance and Network 2.0 efficiencies, despite global trade headwinds and a major contract expiration. FedEx is optimizing U.S. stations and gaining in Europe for growth potential, but freight pressures and higher costs are ongoing concerns.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Dollar General Stock (DG) Loses Its Buy Case After Q4. Here's Why
data.description:
The discount retail giant Dollar General (DG) no longer looks like a clear "buy" after reporting its Q4 results, as the company's Fiscal 2026 guidance points to a slowdown in growth — just as valuations have already expanded sharply over the past year. To that extent, the post-earnings sell-off, in my view, looks justified. In this article, I break down what drove the re-rating, why the setup is now shifting, and what would need to change for my current Hold thesis to turn constructive again.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

While Dollar General did deliver a very solid Q4, beating estimates across all key metrics, the market was clearly expecting more — either a clear upward revision or a stronger forward outlook. Instead, management struck a more cautious tone, pointing to slower growth and more modest margin expansion throughout 2026, which ultimately dampened sentiment. Even after the recent correction, DG still trades closer to historical multiples, where I see the risk/reward as less compelling.
data.author: Bernard Zambonin

data.title: Top Truist Analyst Raises Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Price Target on GTC Updates
data.description:
Nvidia's (NVDA) product updates and new partnership announcements at the GTC event have not driven any notable rise in its stock. However, the event has reinforced the bullish thesis of several top analysts. On Wednesday, top Truist analyst William Stein reiterated a Buy rating on Nvidia stock and raised his price target to $287 from $283, citing key updates from the chip giant on the second day of the event and its strong data center backlog.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

Truist Analyst Highlights Key Takeaways from Nvidia's GTC Event

Stein highlighted three key takeaways from the second day of the GTC event, "The Super Bowl of AI:"

  • Management called 2025 "the year of inference," with the market transitioning from training-centric builds to production inference at scale. The 5-star analyst noted three demand drivers mentioned by the company: generative AI accelerating demand for tokens; OpenClaw (which NVDA supports through NeMoClaw) creating a "ChatGPT moment" for Agentic AI, which is expected to further boost demand for tokens; and the rapid growth in the number and depth of physical AI projects (autonomous driving and humanoid robotics).
  • Inference requires dominance in "tokenomics" (tokens/sec/watt), and the semiconductor company is addressing this requirement with new systems based on its rack-scale Vera Rubin platform and a heterogeneous design that enables customers to mix and match five different resource racks.
  • Noting Nvidia's update about revenue visibility of $1 trillion based on orders for Blackwell and Vera Rubin systems through 2027 (up from $500 billion through 2026 last year), Stein stated that the company's backlog is growing amid rising demand and better products. Given that the Street's data center revenue estimate is $950 billion from 2025-2027, Stein sees "at least modest upside" in 2026 and 2027. Considering management's comments, the analyst raised his calendar year 2027 data center revenue estimate to $468 billion from $439 billion and EPS estimate to $11.48 from $10.12.

Is NVDA a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Given continued momentum in demand for Nvidia's AI GPUs (graphics processing units), Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on NVDA stock based on 40 Buys and only one Hold rating. The average NVDA stock price target of $274.16 indicates 52% upside potential from current levels. NVDA stock has risen 56% over the past year but is down more than 3% year-to-date.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/19/26 – Stock Futures Slump as Iran War Intensifies, Fed Warns of Inflation
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell early Thursday as the Israel-Iran war intensified, pushing oil prices higher. The U.S. Federal Reserve kept the interest rate unchanged yesterday but warned of higher inflationary pressure. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.56%, 0.42%, and 0.38%, respectively, at 5:36 a.m. EST on March 19.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the time of writing, the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was up 3.29% at $115.48 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) traded near $96.81 per barrel.

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed lower after Fed Chair Jerome Powell cautioned that surging oil prices could stoke inflation fears. The Dow Jones fell to a fresh 2026 low, tumbling 1.63%, while the S&P 500 shed 1.36% and the Nasdaq dropped 1.46%.

After markets closed, Micron Technology (MU) shares fell more than 4% despite beating fiscal Q2 sales and earnings expectations. The chipmaker's revenues nearly tripled, fueled by a memory chip supply shortage, and guided above consensus for Q3. However, its increased capex guidance left investors worried about returns.

Looking ahead to economic reports, data on building permits, new home sales, weekly jobless claims, and Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index are due today.

Trade QQQ with leverage

On the earnings front, notable companies reporting today include Alibaba (BABA), FedEx (FDX), and Accenture (ACN).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.28%. Additionally, the Spot Gold U.S. dollar price traded around $4,720 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened lower amid the escalating war. Chip companies are already facing shortages as the war has halted freight routes.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Lower

Asia-Pacific markets traded lower on March 19 as global investors digested the impact of the Israel-Iran war.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 2.02%. In China, the Shanghai Composite dropped 1.39%, while the Shenzhen Component declined 2.36%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei plunged 3.38%, with the Topix losing 2.91%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth
